text: And now you can see edited surgical video.
Our standard approach is uniportal VATS.
For placation of diaphragm we use sixth intercostal space.
Please note the pulmonary ligament which is visible only when the diaphragm is pushed down.
Later this will be used as a landmark to appreciate effect of our placation.
We are starting to place sutures.
The first line of the sutures is placed in the most distant part of diaphragm.
Our plan is to put three main rows of the sutures and then place additional sutures between the main ones.
The suture material is Etibond 2.0 with two needles and we will reinforce the sutures with ledges on the both sides.
As you can see direction of the suture line is from anterior to posterior and then back.
As you can see, this technique does not require any wristed instruments or CO2 insufflation.
After completing the first row the suture is tied and cut.
For this suture we are using endoscopic knot pusher.
We continue with the next line.
This suture is placed approximately at the midline of diaphragm and it will cross the highest point of the elevation The video goes fast forward to save your time
For most of these sutures, knots are pushed with finger to control tension of the diaphragm except the first suture because it is located too deep.
Here you can already appreciate position of diaphragm after the first two sutures.
After completing each suture you can see the diaphragm shrinking.
Now we place additional sutures between the main ones.
These ushers also are reinforced with pledges.
Foldings of the diaphragm are pushed down to create a more or less even surface.
Another suture here.
It is very important to place the same sutures on the lateral aspect of diaphragm.
The suturing here is a bit more difficult because of the different view angle.
Here we speed up video again.
now this is final view now you can appreciate the position of intention of the diaphragm the pulmonary ligament is visible without any pushing of the diaphragm
Now we are placing extrapleural catheter for post-operative pain control.
The EZ pump device will be connected to this catheter and a local anesthetic will be infused.
Usually this catheter is removed together with the chest tube.
We use single 20 French drain that is placed through the wound.
